>

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web-requires-channel-dsl--functions"></a>

## Functions

[secure](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-requires-channel-dsl/secure.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [secure](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-requires-channel-dsl/secure.html)(pattern: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html), attribute: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html) = "REQUIRES\_SECURE\_CHANNEL")
>
> fun [secure](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-requires-channel-dsl/secure.html)(pattern: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html), servletPath: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html), attribute: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html) = "REQUIRES\_SECURE\_CHANNEL")
>
> Adds a request authorization rule for an endpoint matching the provided pattern. If Spring MVC is not an the classpath, it will use an ant matcher. If Spring MVC is on the classpath, it will use an MVC matcher. The MVC will use the same rules that Spring MVC uses for matching. For example, often times a mapping of the path "/path" will match on "/path", "/path/", "/path.html", etc. If the current request will not be processed by Spring MVC, a reasonable default using the pattern as an ant pattern will be used.
>
> fun [secure](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-requires-channel-dsl/secure.html)(matches: RequestMatcher = AnyRequestMatcher.INSTANCE, attribute: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html) = "REQUIRES\_SECURE\_CHANNEL")
>
> Adds a channel security rule.

---

> [!NOTE]

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> var [permitAll](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/permit-all.html): [Boolean](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-boolean/index.html)?
>
> whether to grant access to the urls for [failureUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/failure-url.html) as well as for the [HttpSecurityBuilder](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-http-security-builder/index.html), the [loginPage](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/login-page.html) and [loginProcessingUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/login-processing-url.html) for every user

[relyingPartyRegistrationRepository](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/relying-party-registration-repository.html)

>
> the RelyingPartyRegistrationRepository of relying parties, each party representing a service provider, SP and this host, and identity provider, IDP pair that communicate with each other.

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web-saml2-dsl--functions"></a>

## Functions

[defaultSuccessUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/default-success-url.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [defaultSuccessUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/default-success-url.html)(defaultSuccessUrl: [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html), alwaysUse: [Boolean](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-boolean/index.html))
>
> Specifies where users will be redirected after authenticating successfully if they have not visited a secured page prior to authenticating or [alwaysUse](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/default-success-url.html) is true.

[permitAll](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/permit-all.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [permitAll](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/permit-all.html)()
>
> Grants access to the urls for [failureUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/failure-url.html) as well as for the [HttpSecurityBuilder](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-http-security-builder/index.html), the [loginPage](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/login-page.html) and [loginProcessingUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-saml2-dsl/login-processing-url.html) for every user.

[sessionConcurrency](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-session-management-dsl/session-concurrency.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [sessionConcurrency](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web/-session-management-dsl/session-concurrency.html)(sessionConcurrencyConfig: [SessionConcurrencyDsl](#api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session-session-concurrency-dsl).() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html))
>
> Controls the behaviour of multiple sessions for a user.

> [!NOTE]
> var [includeSubDomains](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.headers/-http-public-key-pinning-dsl/include-sub-domains.html): [Boolean](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-boolean/index.html)?
>

>

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var pins : Map < String , String > ? the value for the pin- directive of the Public-Key-Pins header.**
>

[reportOnly](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.headers/-http-public-key-pinning-dsl/report-only.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var reportOnly : Boolean ? if true, the browser should not terminate the connection with the server.**
>

[reportUri](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.headers/-http-public-key-pinning-dsl/report-uri.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var reportUri : String ? the URI to which the browser should report pin validation failures.**
>

>
> the RequestMatcher used to determine if the "Strict-Transport-Security" header should be added. If true the header is added, else the header is not added.

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver-d-po-p-dsl--properties"></a>

## Properties

[authenticationConverter](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver/-d-po-p-dsl/authentication-converter.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> var [authenticationConverter](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver/-d-po-p-dsl/authentication-converter.html): AuthenticationConverter?
>
> the AuthenticationConverter used when attempting to extract a DPoP-bound access token from HttpServletRequest to an instance of DPoPAuthenticationToken used for authenticating the DPoP-protected resource request. The default is DPoPAuthenticationConverter.

[authenticationFailureHandler](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver/-d-po-p-dsl/authentication-failure-handler.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> var [authenticationFailureHandler](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver/-d-po-p-dsl/authentication-failure-handler.html): AuthenticationFailureHandler?
>
> the AuthenticationFailureHandler used for handling a failed DPoP-protected resource request. The default is AuthenticationEntryPointFailureHandler with DPoPAuthenticationEntryPoint.

[authenticationSuccessHandler](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver/-d-po-p-dsl/authentication-success-handler.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> var [authenticationSuccessHandler](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver/-d-po-p-dsl/authentication-success-handler.html): AuthenticationSuccessHandler?
>
> the AuthenticationSuccessHandler used for handling an authenticated DPoP-protected resource request.

[requestMatcher](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.oauth2.resourceserver/-d-po-p-dsl/request-matcher.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var requestMatcher : RequestMatcher ? the RequestMatcher used when matching the HttpServletRequest to a DPoP-protected resource request.**
>

[expiredSessionStrategy](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/expired-session-strategy.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var expiredSessionStrategy : SessionInformationExpiredStrategy ? determines the behaviour when an expired session is detected.**
>

[expiredUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/expired-url.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> var [expiredUrl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/expired-url.html): [String](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-string/index.html)?
>
> the URL to redirect to if a user tries to access a resource and their session has been expired due to too many sessions for the current user.

[maximumSessions](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/maximum-sessions.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var maximumSessions : Int ? controls the maximum number of sessions for a user.**
>

[maxSessionsPreventsLogin](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/max-sessions-prevents-login.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> var [maxSessionsPreventsLogin](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/max-sessions-prevents-login.html): [Boolean](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-boolean/index.html)?
>
> if true, prevents a user from authenticating when the [maximumSessions](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/maximum-sessions.html) has been reached. Otherwise (default), the user who authenticates is allowed access and an existing user's session is expired.

[sessionRegistry](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/session-registry.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var sessionRegistry : SessionRegistry ? the SessionRegistry implementation used.**
>

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session-session-concurrency-dsl--functions"></a>

## Functions

[maximumSessions](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-concurrency-dsl/maximum-sessions.html)

> [!NOTE]
> **fun maximumSessions ( max : SessionLimit )**

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **constructor ( )**

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session-session-fixation-dsl--functions"></a>

## Functions

[changeSessionId](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-fixation-dsl/change-session-id.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [changeSessionId](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-fixation-dsl/change-session-id.html)()
>
> Specifies that the Servlet container-provided session fixation protection should be used. When a session authenticates, the Servlet method HttpServletRequest.changeSessionId is called to change the session ID and retain all session attributes.

[migrateSession](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-fixation-dsl/migrate-session.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [migrateSession](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-fixation-dsl/migrate-session.html)()
>
> Specifies that a new session should be created and the session attributes from the original HttpSession should be retained.

[newSession](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-fixation-dsl/new-session.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [newSession](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-fixation-dsl/new-session.html)()
>
> Specifies that a new session should be created, but the session attributes from the original HttpSession should not be retained.

[none](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.session/-session-fixation-dsl/none.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **fun none ( ) Specifies that no session fixation protection should be enabled.**
>

>

>

>
> Allows configuring the [ServerHttpSecurity](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-http-security/index.html) to only be invoked when matching the provided ServerWebExchangeMatcher.

> [!NOTE]
> class [ServerHttpsRedirectDsl](#api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-https-redirect-dsl)
>
> A Kotlin DSL to configure [ServerHttpSecurity](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-http-security/index.html) HTTPS redirection rules using idiomatic Kotlin code.
>
>

>
> #### Author
>
> Eleftheria Stein
>
>

>
> #### Since
>
> 5.4

Members

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-https-redirect-dsl--constructors"></a>

## Constructors

[ServerHttpsRedirectDsl](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-https-redirect-dsl/-server-https-redirect-dsl.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **constructor ( )**
>

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-https-redirect-dsl--properties"></a>

## Properties

[portMapper](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-https-redirect-dsl/port-mapper.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> **var portMapper : PortMapper ? the PortMapper that specifies a custom HTTPS port to redirect to.**
>

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-https-redirect-dsl--functions"></a>

## Functions

[httpsRedirectWhen](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-https-redirect-dsl/https-redirect-when.html)

Link copied to clipboard

> [!NOTE]
> fun [httpsRedirectWhen](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-https-redirect-dsl/https-redirect-when.html)(redirectMatcherFunction: (ServerWebExchange) -> [Boolean](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-boolean/index.html))
>
> fun [httpsRedirectWhen](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-https-redirect-dsl/https-redirect-when.html)(vararg redirectMatchers: ServerWebExchangeMatcher)
>
> Configures when this filter should redirect to https. If invoked multiple times, whether a matcher or a function is provided, only the last redirect rule will apply and all previous rules will be overridden.

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-xss-protection-dsl--server-xss-protection-dsl"></a>

# ServerXssProtectionDsl

> [!NOTE]
> class [ServerXssProtectionDsl](#api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-xss-protection-dsl)
>
> A Kotlin DSL to configure the [ServerHttpSecurity](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-http-security/index.html) XSS protection header using idiomatic Kotlin code.
>
>

>
> #### Author
>
> Eleftheria Stein
>
>

>

>
> 5.4

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-invoke"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/invoke.html -->

<!-- page_index: 294 -->

<a id="api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-invoke--invoke"></a>

# invoke

> [!NOTE]
> operator fun [ServerHttpSecurity](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-http-security/index.html).[invoke](#api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-invoke)(httpConfiguration: [ServerHttpSecurityDsl](#api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-http-security-dsl).() -> [Unit](https://kotlinlang.org/api/core/kotlin-stdlib/kotlin/-unit/index.html)): SecurityWebFilterChain
>
> Configures [ServerHttpSecurity](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-http-security/index.html) using a [ServerHttpSecurity Kotlin DSL](#api-kotlin-spring-security-config-org.springframework.security.config.web.server-server-http-security-dsl).
>
> Example:
>
> ```kotlin
> @Configuration @EnableWebFluxSecurity class SecurityConfig {
> @Bean fun springWebFilterChain(http: ServerHttpSecurity): SecurityWebFilterChain {return http {authorizeExchange {authorize("/public", permitAll) authorize(anyExchange, authenticated)}}}}
> ```
>
> Content copied to clipboard
>
>

>

>
>

> 5.4
>
>

>
> #### Parameters
>
> httpConfiguration
>
> the configurations to apply to [ServerHttpSecurity](https://docs.spring.io/spring-security/reference/api/kotlin/spring-security-config/org.springframework.security.config.web.server/-server-http-security/index.html)
